name: Crab Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Make cracker crust by combining 1 cup crushed Ritz crackers and 3 tablespoons melted butter in a 9-inch spring form pan, lined with parchment paper.
2. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 10 minutes until set.
3. Turn the oven down to 325°F (160°C).
4. In a large bowl, beat cream cheese with an electric mixer until smooth and fluffy using 2 packages of 8 oz cream cheese.
5. Add 3 medium eggs, 1/4 cup sour cream, 1 teaspoon lemon juice, 2 teaspoons grated onion, 1/2 teaspoon Old Bay seasoning, 1 drop Tabasco sauce, and 1/8 teaspoon black pepper to the bowl. Mix until well combined.
6. Stir in 1 cup crab meat, shredding it thoroughly while removing shells.
7. Pour the mixture into the cooled crust.
8. Bake at 325°F (160°C) for 50 minutes or until set.
9. Remove from oven and run a knife around the edge of the cake to loosen it from the pan's side.
10. Cool on a wire rack, then remove sides of the pan. Spread half a cup of sour cream over the top.
- This versatile dish serves warm for brunch with six people or cold as canapés for 12 or more.
